findings.name: vision_clipped_content
findings.evidence: Mobile render has horizontal page overflow with visible horizontal scrollbar; several long pill labels run past the right edge of their cards/viewport and are clipped, making mobile content partially offscreen.

findings.name: vision_clipped_content
findings.evidence: Mobile viewport shows horizontal overflow/scrollbar and the first finding card's long label extends past the right edge, leaving content offscreen.

findings.name: vision_clipped_content
findings.evidence: In the mobile deep view, the milestone criterion tables render as horizontally scrollable boxes with most table columns/content offscreen and large blank table areas visible. The surrounding prose remains readable, but the table content is effectively clipped in the mobile layout.

findings.name: vision_clipped_content
findings.evidence: A second milestone criterion table has the same mobile rendering issue: the table is too wide for the card, leaving most columns offscreen behind a horizontal scrollbar and only a clipped fragment of the row visible.

findings.name: vision_chart_malformed
findings.evidence: The weekly trend chart contains stray disconnected colored point/line fragments near the lower middle of the plot area, separate from the intended rising line series and bars. This could be mistaken for extra low data points.

findings.name: vision_chart_malformed
findings.evidence: The small trend chart includes disconnected colored marker fragments near the lower portion of the plot, visually separate from the main line trajectories and bars.
